reasoning: Tool correctly fires for hanabi intent and returns 386 fireworks events from a real Wikipedia-backed dataset. Coverage is excellent. However most items lack English names, dates, prefecture data, and coordinates — the agent cannot build a usable itinerary from names-only Japanese strings. The raw list is correct but practically thin for an English-language query.
missing_data: Dates/months for each fireworks event, English names for most entries, coordinates missing on most items, prefecture missing on most entries.
improvement_hint: Add month/date field from Wikidata P585 or description parsing; prioritise items with coordinates and prefecture in ranking; translate top-50 name_ja to name_en automatically.

reasoning: Identical dataset as L3-01 but served with lang=ja, which is appropriate for a Japanese query. Same structural weakness: most items lack dates, coordinates, and prefecture. The ja-native user would still struggle to select among 386 unlocalised results. Response is semantically correct but thin on actionable metadata.
missing_data: Event dates/months, prefecture coverage on majority of items, coordinates.
improvement_hint: Same as L3-01: surface date/month and prefecture fields; allow prefecture filter on get_festivals to narrow list to actionable subset.

reasoning: Query is about snow festivals in Japan (primarily Hokkaido). Tool called get_festivals(prefecture=Hokkaido) and returned 0 festivals items; the response body is entirely filled with national UNESCO heritage entries unrelated to snow festivals. The Sapporo Snow Festival and Asahikawa Winter Festival are well-known but absent. Complete retrieval failure for the intended topic.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival, Asahikawa Winter Festival, any yukimatsuri entries from Hokkaido.
wrong_data: UNESCO entries (Dainichido Bugaku, Hayachine Kagura etc.) are irrelevant to the query and may mislead the agent.
improvement_hint: Add yukimatsuri / 雪祭り as a kind_tag in the festival corpus; ingest Hokkaido winter festival data from official sources; improve prefecture-filtered fallback so irrelevant national heritage is not surfaced when count=0.

reasoning: Query is about experiencing rural life in Japan (田舎体験). Tool called get_japan_heritage with no filters, returning 104 Japan Heritage stories plus canonical heritage sites. Top results include Nagoya Castle and Sannai-Maruyama Site — urban/archaeological, not rural life. Intent mismatch: Japan Heritage stories can cover rural themes but the agent retrieved a generic unfiltered list rather than targeting kominka, satoyama, or noson-themed records.
missing_data: Satoyama / kominka / farmstay experiences; Japan Heritage stories specifically themed on rural life such as those in Niigata or Shiga.
improvement_hint: Support theme_filter param for rural/satoyama in get_japan_heritage; or guide agent to search_area with q=里山 or q=農村 before calling this tool.

reasoning: Query asks for autumn foliage spots (단풍) in Korean. Tool searched for 紅葉 which is correct, and returned 147 results including genuine foliage parks. However top hits are named '紅葉谷川庭園砂防施設' (a sediment-control facility) and local parks — not the famous koyo destinations (Nikko, Kyoto, Arashiyama). Ranking places niche engineering infrastructure above iconic foliage spots, reducing usefulness. No Korean translations surfaced.
missing_data: Iconic foliage destinations like Nikko, Arashiyama, Korankei. Korean name translations. Best season/month data.
improvement_hint: Demote sediment-control/civil-engineering items when the intent is scenic foliage; boost sakura_meisho_100 and known foliage parks by tier; ensure lang=ko surfaces Korean name fields.

reasoning: User explicitly wants a quiet, non-touristy meditation place, not a touristy temple. Tool searched 修行 and returned shugyo-ji in urban Tokyo (Suginami), a DMO event entry for a special Rinzai-ji visit experience, and a ninja experience (忍者の森) — the ninja entry is clearly off-target. The constraint 'not touristy' is ignored. Results include some genuine meditation-related entries but miss mountain retreat temples like Eiheiji or Koya-san remote areas.
missing_data: Remote mountain temples with zazen/shugyo programs (Eiheiji, Tofukuji, Koyasan sub-temples). Quiet rural temple options.
wrong_data: Ninja experience result is irrelevant noise for meditation intent.
improvement_hint: Add a 'shugyo/zazen experience' kind_tag for temples offering retreat programs; filter out ninja/samurai activities from meditation queries; surface rural rather than urban temple results when quiet is specified.

reasoning: User wants to see northern lights (aurora borealis) in Japan. Aurora sightings are extremely rare and geographically limited to far northern Hokkaido. The tool returned observatories, national parks in Okinawa, and a ropeway — none specifically for aurora viewing. The response does not clarify that Japan is not an aurora destination and fails to surface the one valid location (Rikubetsu or northernmost Hokkaido). Highly misleading for an agent trying to help.
missing_data: Honest clarification that Japan is a poor aurora destination; Rikubetsu dark-sky site in Hokkaido as the best slim chance; recommendation to visit Iceland/Norway instead.
wrong_data: Okinawa national parks, ropeway, and planetariums are entirely irrelevant to aurora borealis viewing.
improvement_hint: Handle impossible/edge-case queries: add a corpus note for aurora=rare_in_japan with redirect to Hokkaido dark-sky sites; surface disambiguation content when intent cannot be satisfied by available data.

reasoning: User wants to sleep in a 100-year-old traditional house (古民家). Tool called get_hotels(Gifu) and returned onsen ryokan (秘湯を守る会), an Airbnb apartment, and a hostel — none explicitly identified as 古民家 (kominka). Shirakawa-go in Gifu has famous gassho-zukuri farmhouses and kominka stays but they are not in the results. The tool choice is reasonable but lacks kominka-specific data and the ranking doesn't address the constraint.
missing_data: Kominka / machiya guesthouses in Shirakawa-go, Hida Furukawa, or Takayama old town. Lodging age/heritage information.
improvement_hint: Tag accommodation records with kominka/machiya/gassho designation where available from official sources; add heritage_building field to hotel records so agents can filter by traditional-building type.

reasoning: Thai query asks for lesser-known (穴場) cherry-blossom spots away from tourists. Tool searched 桜 and returned 201 results including genuine sakura spots. However top results include the highly-tourist Himeji Castle, Kumamoto Castle, and Osaka Mint — famous crowded spots contradicting the 'not crowded' constraint. The system serves popular spots rather than lesser-known ones. Lang=th not reflected in output names.
missing_data: Lesser-known sakura spots (rural prefectures, low-prominence_score parks). Thai name translations.
improvement_hint: When agent passes an 'uncrowded/hidden' modifier, de-prioritise high-prominence_score and nihon_100_meijo items; surface parks with sakura_meisho_100 kind but lower prominence scores; provide Thai translations.

reasoning: Query asks for dramatic landscapes for photography. Tool searched 絶景 and returned 53 results. Top results are DMO article pages (itinerary guides from Ehime, Niigata), not individual named viewpoints. These are useful but require the agent to parse DMO article text rather than using structured spot records. The response works but is impractical — no GPS coordinates, opening hours, or direct spot names for the top hits.
missing_data: Named individual viewpoints with coordinates (e.g. Tateyama Kurobe, Cape Toi, Aogashima). Structured spot data rather than DMO article URLs.
improvement_hint: Index individual 絶景 viewpoints as spot records with coordinates; surface wikidata-backed scenic viewpoints (展望台, 絶景) at higher tier before DMO article pages.

reasoning: Arabic query asks for outdoor hot springs in nature, not in hotels. Tool searched 露天風呂 and returned outdoor bath spots including Hochizenbou Onsen and Haraikawa Onsen with nature settings — partially relevant. However constraint 'not in hotels' is not handled; most results are hotel/ryokan bathing facilities. No Arabic translations in output. Some results are genuine open-air natural baths. Partially useful but constraint compliance is weak.
missing_data: Wild/free outdoor onsen (notenboro) like Nyuto Onsen, Shikanoyu, or wild-river baths in Hokkaido. Arabic translations.
improvement_hint: Add a tag for notenboro (wild outdoor bath accessible without lodging stay); distinguish hotel-attached rotenburo from standalone nature baths; surface Arabic translations for lang=ar.

reasoning: Query is specifically about swimming with wild dolphins. Tool searched イルカ and returned: a Kyoto cultural ambassador page (first result, completely irrelevant), an Ishikawa aquarium with captive dolphin shows, and a fish market. The one genuine wild-dolphin swim location in Japan (Mikurajima/Ogasawara) is absent. First result is pure retrieval noise. Intent is not addressed.
missing_data: Mikurajima Island wild dolphin snorkeling (Izu Islands), Ogasawara Islands dolphin encounters — both famous for wild dolphin swimming.
wrong_data: Kyoto cultural ambassador page as first result is a clear false positive with no dolphin relevance.
improvement_hint: Tag Mikurajima and Ogasawara dolphin-swim spots with dolphin_swim kind; improve BM25/vector retrieval to de-rank pages that contain イルカ only incidentally (aquarium shows, fish markets).

reasoning: Query asks about traditional architecture districts (重伝建). Tool called get_japan_heritage with no filter and returned top_canonical_heritage: Nagoya Castle, Sannai-Maruyama Site, Ichijodani Ruins — prestigious heritage sites but not traditional townscape preservation districts (重要伝統的建造物群保存地区). The correct data exists in the corpus (search_area q=重伝建 would surface Naraijuku, Tsumago etc. tagged preservation_district) but wrong tool was used.
missing_data: Preservation districts (重伝建): Shirakawa-go, Narai-juku, Tsumago-juku, Uchiko, Gion Shirakawa. These are tagged preservation_district in wikidata but not surfaced here.
improvement_hint: Route traditional-architecture queries to search_area with kind_filter=preservation_district or add a theme_filter for machiya/townscape to get_japan_heritage; the tool_scope_note itself hints at this redirection.

reasoning: Vietnamese query asks for Ainu culture experience in Hokkaido. Tool searched アイヌ and correctly returned National Ainu Museum (Upopoy in Shiraoi), old Ainu Museum (Porotokotan), Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum, Hokkaido Ainu Center, and Kawamura Kaneto Ainu Museum — all legitimate, well-sourced Hokkaido Ainu institutions. Coordinates, websites, and visit-time estimates present. Comprehensive and accurate response.
missing_data: Vietnamese name translations; Ainu cultural experience programs (craft workshops, dance performances at Upopoy).
improvement_hint: Add Vietnamese (vi) translations for top Ainu institution names; link to experience-program data from Upopoy official site to improve practical usefulness.

reasoning: Query asks for cliff-carved Buddhist statues (magaibutsu). Top results are Usuki Stone Buddhas (National Treasure, prominence 0.9), Oya-dera in Tochigi (prominence 0.9), Kiyomizu Magaibutsu in Kagoshima — all accurate, highly relevant, with heritage designations and coordinates. Coverage spans Oita, Tochigi, Kagoshima, Kanagawa, Fukuoka. Excellent retrieval for a niche query.
missing_data: Opening hours and admission fees for top sites. English descriptions for lower-tier entries.
improvement_hint: Enrich top magaibutsu entries with opening hours and fee data from official municipal sources; add English description for Usuki Stone Buddhas specifically.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for day-trip volcano hikes from Tokyo. Tool searched 火山 and returned Mt. Iwate (Iwate, 500km), Mt. Unzen (Nagasaki, 1000km+), Lake Towada (Aomori, 600km), Lake Mashu (Hokkaido) — none reachable as a day trip from Tokyo. Mt. Fuji appeared second and is the only Tokyo day-trip volcano, but the distance/feasibility constraint is completely ignored. The result set spans all of Japan with no geographic filtering.
missing_data: Hakone (Owakudani active volcanic area, 90min from Tokyo), Mt. Fuji highlighted as primary day-trip option, Izu Peninsula volcanoes.
improvement_hint: Implement geographic proximity filtering in search_area or plan_feasibility_check; when a base-city constraint ('from Tokyo') is detected, rank results by travel time from the specified hub city.

reasoning: Indonesian query asks for freshly-made tofu at a temple (寺 豆腐). Tool searched 豆腐 and returned MAFF GI products (frozen tofu, Iki island tofu), and artisan tofu shops near Mt. Kongo — useful for tofu enthusiasts but no temple-tofu experiences (shojin ryori, koya-dofu at Koyasan, or Daitokuji tofu kaiseki). The 'at a temple' constraint is ignored. No Indonesian translations.
missing_data: Koyasan shojin ryori with tofu, Daitokuji Ikkyu-an kaiseki, Nishiki market Kyoto tofu experiences tied to temples.
improvement_hint: Tag shojin-ryori establishments near or within temple complexes; add temple_dining kind_tag; surface these when tofu+temple intent is detected. Support Indonesian translations.

reasoning: Query asks for fishing villages 'frozen in time' (nostalgic, preserved). Tool searched 漁港 and returned a Shizuoka sakura-ebi restaurant guide, Itoman Fishing Port (Okinawa), a fish market in Niigata, and a notice about a restricted fishing area — functional fishing ports rather than preserved traditional villages. Places like Irifunecho (Hakodate), Ine no Funaya (Kyoto), or Tomo no Ura would fit intent but are absent.
missing_data: Ine no Funaya (Kyoto funaya boat houses, preservation district), Tomo no Ura (Hiroshima, Edo-era port), Hakodate Motomachi fishing heritage area.
improvement_hint: Add kind_tag fishing_village and preservation_district crosslinks; index Ine no Funaya as a designated preservation district with kind=funaya; improve semantic matching for 'frozen in time' to surface heritage-protected fishing settlements.

reasoning: Query asks about learning traditional Japanese paper-making from a master (和紙). Tool called get_local_specialty(category=craft) and returned 231 craft items starting with Nibutani Attus (Ainu textile), Oitama Tsumugi (silk), Ugo Shina Cloth — none of these are washi. The METI list does include washi designations (Echizen Washi, Mino Washi, etc.) but they are not surfaced in the truncated top items. No experience/workshop data returned.
missing_data: Echizen Washi (Fukui), Mino Washi (Gifu), Ogawa Hosokawa Washi (UNESCO) — all METI-designated. Workshop/experience programs from official craft centers.
improvement_hint: Support q= keyword filtering in get_local_specialty so agent can pass q=和紙 to retrieve paper-related crafts directly; add experience_available flag when official craft workshop is documented.

reasoning: Query asks for jidaigeki (period drama) scenery. Tool searched 宿場 and returned Narai-juku, Seki-juku, Tsumago-juku — all preservation districts with Important Preservation District designation — exactly the right results. DMO articles about Kiso Valley add context. Coordinates, heritage designations, and website data present for top attractions. Strong intent match.
missing_data: A few more shukuba-machi across the Tokaido (Hakone-shuku area). Opening/visiting information.
improvement_hint: Expand coverage of Tokaido post-town preservation districts; add typical visit duration and seasonal highlights to shukuba-tagged attractions.

reasoning: Query asks for uncrowded beaches. Tool searched ビーチ and returned 115 results. Top results are Naminoue Beach (Naha, Okinawa — very popular urban beach), Tropical Beach (Okinawa), Sunayama Beach (Miyakojima). These are well-known tourist beaches, not hidden ones. The 'not crowded' constraint is ignored. Many valid less-visited beaches on the Sea of Japan coast or Yamagata/Akita are absent from top results.
missing_data: Less-visited beaches: Shimane coast, Niigata Kakuda beach, Iwate coastal beaches, Sea of Japan beaches in Tottori with hakusha_seishou_100 tag.
improvement_hint: When 'not crowded' / 'hidden' modifier is detected, down-rank high-tourism Okinawa beaches and surface lower-prominence_score beach results, especially those tagged hakusha_seishou_100 in non-Okinawa prefectures.

reasoning: Query asks for small old-school izakaya yokocho drinking alleys. Tool searched 横丁 and returned yokocho-tagged results including Haikara't Yokocho (Yamaguchi amusement park), Naniwa Kuishimbo Yokocho (Osaka museum attraction) — themed tourist recreations, not authentic local drinking alleys. The genuine old-school yokocho (Omoide Yokocho, Koenji, Yurakucho under-the-tracks) are in Wikidata but buried below tourist-attraction-park type results.
missing_data: Omoide Yokocho (Tokyo), Nishiki Koji alley references, authentic non-tourist yokocho areas.
improvement_hint: Distinguish yokocho tagged as tourist_attraction+park (recreation) from authentic food/drinking alleys in ranking; de-rank theme-park yokocho recreations when user intent is local/authentic drinking experience.

reasoning: German query asks for train travel through rural Japan. Tool searched ローカル線 and returned Sagano Scenic Line, Oigawa Main Line (steam locomotive), Ueda Dentetsu Bessho Line with good narrative, Oga Line (Akita), Ouhu Main Line — all legitimate scenic rural railways. DMO article about Nagano local-line art travel adds rich context. Good geographic spread across Japan. Practical, accurate, and well-structured.
missing_data: German translations for station names; timetable pointers. Asa Kaigan Railway or Watarase Keikoku scenic railway could be added.
improvement_hint: Add German (de) translations for top railway names; include a scenic_railway kind_tag to enable direct filtering; surface Watarase Keikoku and Asa Kaigan as additional scenic examples.

reasoning: Query asks for firefly-viewing spots in summer. Tool searched ホタル and returned ホタルの里 park (Hyogo), Firefly Museum of Toyota Town (Yamaguchi with website), Kitakyushu hotaru observation page, and an excellent Yamaguchi Toyoda-cho ホタル舟 (firefly boat) description with pricing, dates (June), and booking details. The Kanazawa Kenroku-en firefly observation event also appears. Practical, accurate, and specific.
missing_data: Dates are 2025-specific (may be outdated by event); Genji-botaru designated natural monument sites in other prefectures (Mie, Shizuoka). Coordinates for top parks.
improvement_hint: Add coordinates to ホタルの里 park; normalise event-date fields so that year-specific pricing/dates are flagged as potentially outdated; surface more genji-botaru habitat designated sites.

reasoning: Russian query asks where to see cranes in winter in Japan. Top result is Izumi crane migration grounds (Kagoshima) — exactly the world's largest crane wintering site, with Natural Monument and Ramsar designations, coordinates, and a detailed English description (10,000 cranes Oct–March). Kushiro Wetland (Hokkaido) for tancho cranes also appears. Slightly diluted by many municipality name matches (Tsuruoka, Tsuruga etc.) that are noise. Core results are excellent.
missing_data: Russian translations for top results. Tsurui-Ito Tancho Sanctuary in Hokkaido (red-crowned cranes) as a second specific site.
improvement_hint: Filter out pure municipality name-matches (鶴見区, 真鶴町) when wildlife-viewing intent is detected; add Russian translations for crane-related sites; surface Tsurui Tancho sanctuary explicitly as a crane_wintering kind entry.
